description: Base assets + sources config for BlogWatcher v2 (with common extract rules)
common_extract_rules:
RULES — APPLY TO EVERY ENTRY:
(R1) ASSET WHITELIST: affected_assets entries MUST come ONLY from this list: [BTC, ETH, SOL, HYPE, LINK, DXY, US10Y, VIX, BTC.D, GOLD, SILVER, OIL, NATGAS, NDX, SPX, CORN, COFFEE, COCOA]. Map common aliases: Bitcoin=BTC, Ethereum=ETH, Solana=SOL, Chainlink=LINK, Dollar Index/USD Index/USD=DXY, 10-year yield/10Y/Treasury yield=US10Y, Volatility Index/Fear Gauge=VIX, Bitcoin Dominance=BTC.D, XAU=GOLD, XAG=SILVER, WTI/Brent/Crude=OIL, Natural Gas/Henry Hub=NATGAS, Nasdaq 100/NQ/Tech 100=NDX, S&P 500/SPY/Dow (proxy)=SPX, Maize/ZC=CORN, Arabica/KC=COFFEE, CC=COCOA. If an asset isn't in the whitelist (e.g. XRP, Copper, individual stocks), DROP it — do NOT remap to similar tickers.
(R2) US-ONLY INDICES: SPX = S&P 500 only. NDX = Nasdaq 100 only. FTSE 100, DAX, CAC, Nikkei, KOSPI, Hang Seng, IBEX → DO NOT map these to SPX or NDX. If a news is about a non-US index AND there is no explicit US spillover, set affected_assets to [] for that entry.
(R3) ASSET INCLUSION: include an asset in affected_assets if it is mentioned by name/ticker OR if the article topic directly and clearly affects it (examples of direct: OPEC cut → OIL; Fed rate change → DXY+US10Y+SPX+NDX; Middle East war → OIL+GOLD+SILVER; central bank gold buying → GOLD+SILVER; CPI print → DXY+US10Y+SPX+NDX+GOLD; geopolitical risk-off → GOLD+SILVER+US10Y bullish, SPX+NDX+BTC bearish). For source-specific pages (Kitco → metals, OilPrice → energy, CoinDesk → crypto), DEFAULT to including the page's natural assets unless the article is clearly off-topic. Better to include relevant assets than to return empty arrays.
(R4) PER-ASSET SENTIMENT — CRITICAL: A single news often impacts assets with OPPOSITE sentiments. Examples: 'DXY weakens, gold rallies' = DXY bearish + GOLD bullish; 'Iran sanctions' = OIL bullish + SPX/NDX bearish + GOLD bullish; 'Fed cuts rates' = DXY bearish + US10Y bearish + SPX/NDX/BTC/ETH bullish + GOLD bullish. When this happens, output MULTIPLE entries — one per sentiment group, sharing the same headline text. ONLY group assets together when they share the SAME sentiment direction.
(R5) KEY LEVELS: include only price levels EXPLICITLY stated in the article text. If none mentioned, set key_levels to []. Do NOT invent or recall levels from memory. Empty is fine.
(R6) IMPACT: 'high' = directly market-moving (FOMC decision, war start, hack >$100M, major data); 'medium' = notable (analyst calls, secondary data, sector news); 'low' = minor/contextual.
(R7) SENTIMENT VOCABULARY: use ONLY 'bullish' | 'bearish' | 'neutral'. Do not use 'positive', 'negative', 'mixed', 'up', 'down' — those will be rejected.
(R8) OUTPUT: valid JSON array only — no markdown fences, no commentary. Aim for 5-7 entries per source if the page has any actionable content. Return [] ONLY when the page is empty or completely off-topic (very rare for our curated sources).

coindesk.label: CoinDesk — crypto markets
coindesk.extract_prompt: Source focus: top 5-7 market-moving crypto headlines from last 12h. Likely assets: BTC, ETH, SOL, HYPE, LINK, BTC.D. Likely categories: macro, regulatory, whale, exchange, ecosystem, defi. SKIP sponsored content and pure opinion pieces. Schema per entry: { headline, summary (1 sentence), affected_assets, sentiment, impact, category, key_levels (optional) }.

theblock.label: The Block — crypto/DeFi markets
theblock.extract_prompt: Source focus: top 5-7 crypto/DeFi headlines from last 12h. PRIORITIZE exchange flows, on-chain data, institutional moves, whale activity, regulatory. Likely assets: BTC, ETH, SOL, HYPE, LINK, BTC.D. Categories: whale, exchange, defi, regulatory, ecosystem, macro. SKIP pure opinion pieces. Schema per entry: { headline, summary, affected_assets, sentiment, impact, category, key_levels (optional) }.

reuters_markets.label: Reuters — financial markets
reuters_markets.extract_prompt: Source focus: top 5-7 financial market headlines from last 12h. Coverage: equities (US: SPX/NDX only — apply R2 strictly), FX (DXY, EUR/USD, USD/JPY → only DXY in whitelist), bonds (US10Y), commodities crossover (GOLD, SILVER, OIL, NATGAS). Categories: macro, fx, bonds, equities, commodities, fed. CRITICAL: foreign indices (FTSE, DAX, Nikkei, Hang Seng) do NOT map to SPX/NDX — see R2. Schema per entry: { headline, summary, affected_assets, sentiment, impact, category, key_levels (optional) }.

reuters_world.label: Reuters — world & geopolitics
reuters_world.extract_prompt: Source focus: top 5-7 geopolitical/world headlines from last 12h that could impact markets. Mapping heuristics (apply only when article CLEARLY implies the linkage): wars/Iran/sanctions → OIL, NATGAS, GOLD, SILVER, CORN (Ukraine grain), DXY; Fed/US policy → DXY, US10Y, SPX, NDX, BTC, ETH; China shock → CORN, COFFEE, COCOA, COPPER (not in list), commodities broadly; risk-off → GOLD, SILVER, US10Y up + SPX/NDX/BTC down; election/trade disputes → DXY, SPX/NDX. Remember R4 (per-asset sentiment) — safe-haven assets often have OPPOSITE sentiment from risk assets in same story. Categories: geopolitics, war, sanctions, election, fed, trade. Schema per entry: { headline, summary, affected_assets, sentiment, impact, category, key_levels (optional) }.

kitco.label: Kitco — gold & silver news
kitco.extract_prompt: Source focus: top 5-7 precious metals headlines from last 12h. Likely assets: GOLD, SILVER. Cross-impact assets (apply R4 — opposite sentiment likely): DXY (USD strength = bearish metals, USD weakness = bullish metals), US10Y (yields up = bearish metals), VIX (risk-off = bullish safe-haven metals). Include central banks, inflation, Fed, USD, ETF flows. Categories: macro, cb_buying, inflation, fed, geopolitics, etf_flows. Schema per entry: { headline, summary, affected_assets, sentiment, impact, category, key_levels (optional, only if explicit in text) }.

oilprice.label: OilPrice.com — crude oil & energy
oilprice.extract_prompt: Source focus: top 5-7 oil/energy headlines from last 12h. Primary assets: OIL, NATGAS. Cross-impact (apply R4 carefully): DXY (oil pricing), SPX/NDX (oil shocks = inflation pressure on equities, R4 may apply), GOLD (oil spike from war = both bullish via inflation/safe haven). Categories: opec, supply, demand, geopolitics, inventory, refining. Key levels only if explicit (e.g. WTI/Brent levels). Schema per entry: { headline, summary, affected_assets, sentiment, impact, category, key_levels (optional) }.

barchart.label: Barchart — commodity news
barchart.extract_prompt: Source focus: top 5-7 commodity/agriculture headlines from last 24h. PRIMARY assets (this is the dedicated softs source): CORN, COFFEE, COCOA. Secondary: OIL, NATGAS, GOLD, SILVER, DXY. Categories: weather, harvest, supply, demand, geopolitics, usda, crop_report. R4 reminder — weather/supply shock typically affects multiple agri assets with SAME sentiment, but DXY usually opposite. Schema per entry: { headline, summary, affected_assets, sentiment, impact, category, key_levels (optional) }.

reuters_commodities.label: Reuters Commodities (fallback for Barchart)
reuters_commodities.extract_prompt: Source focus: top 5-7 commodity headlines from last 24h. Coverage: CORN, COFFEE, COCOA, OIL, NATGAS, GOLD, SILVER, DXY. Categories: supply, demand, geopolitics, weather, usda, opec. Schema per entry: { headline, summary, affected_assets, sentiment, impact, category, key_levels (optional) }.

fed.label: Federal Reserve — press releases
fed.extract_prompt: Source focus: Fed press releases from last 14 days. Likely impacted assets: DXY, US10Y, SPX, NDX, BTC, ETH, GOLD, SILVER. R4 CRITICAL — Fed dovish = bullish for risk (SPX/NDX/BTC/ETH/GOLD/SILVER) + bearish for DXY/US10Y. Fed hawkish = inverse. Categories: fomc, monetary_policy, rates, inflation. Extra field allowed: 'hawkishness' (1-10, 1=very dovish, 10=very hawkish). Schema per entry: { headline, summary, affected_assets, sentiment, impact, category, key_levels (optional), hawkishness (optional) }.

bbc_world.label: BBC News — world (fallback geo)
bbc_world.extract_prompt: Source focus: top 5-7 world events affecting markets from last 12h. Apply same geopolitical mapping as reuters_world: wars/sanctions → OIL/NATGAS/GOLD/SILVER/CORN; risk-off → GOLD/SILVER/US10Y bullish + SPX/NDX/BTC bearish (R4). Categories: war, sanctions, election, trade, china, emerging_markets. Schema per entry: { headline, summary, affected_assets, sentiment, impact, category, key_levels (optional) }.

DXY.name: US Dollar Index
DXY.note: Inversely correlated with most risk assets (BTC, GOLD, equities)

US10Y.name: US 10-Year Treasury Yield
US10Y.note: Real yields drive gold/silver. Above 4.5% = panic mode for risk.

BTC.D.name: Bitcoin Dominance
BTC.D.note: Rising = alts bleeding. Falling = alt season setup.
